title: eragrostis ciliaris subsp. brachystachya
intro: Eragrostis ciliaris subsp. brachystachya, also known as Plains Lovegrass, is a perennial grass native to North America, characterized by its distinctive tufted growth habit and narrow leaves. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Plains Lovegrass include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Plains Lovegrass can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and powdering the plant for use in capsules or tinctures.
